articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.description: In 2017, General Constantine Chiwenga, as head of Zimbabwe’s armed forces, led the military coup which brought President Emmerson Mnangagwa to power and won Chiwenga the vice presidency. The two have since fallen out and are fighting over Mnangagwa’s apparent desire to stay in office for an unconstitutional third term. more.title: UK Commits £500,000 to Fight Gender-Based Violence in Zimbabwe
more.description: [263Chat] The United Kingdom has pledged £500,000 to support efforts to end violence against women and girls in Zimbabwe, reinforcing national programmes focused on prevention and survivor support.

more.title: Ford recalls 1.4 million F-150 pickup trucks to fix a gearshift issue
more.description: NEW YORK — Ford Motor Company is recalling nearly 1.4 million Ford F-150 pickup trucks across the United States after identifying a potentially dangerous gearshift defect that could increase the risk of crashes. more.title: Zimbabwe Seeks Investment in Mineral Value Addition After Raw Export Ban
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] Zimbabwe welcomes more investment in mineral processing and refining as the country shifts from exporting raw minerals to domestic value addition, Vice President Constantino Chiwenga said Wednesday.

more.title: ZiG can now buy fuel in Zimbabwe
more.description: Service stations in Bulawayo have started accepting payments in the Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG), signalling growing confidence in the local currency as Government intensifies efforts to stabilise and entrench its use across the economy. more.title: Liquid Rewrites Its Debt Playbook with Early US$620m Bond Exit and Oversubscribed Re-Issue
more.description: LONDON – Pan-African telecoms group Liquid Intelligent Technologies has retired its US$620 million five-year bond ahead of its September maturity, in a move that underscores both balance sheet discipline and sustained access to global capital markets. more.title: Zambia's Government Takes Possession of Ex-President's Body in Repatriation Row
more.description: [New Zimbabwe] The Zambian government has said it has taken possession of the body of former president Edgar Lungu - who died in South Africa 10 months ago - against the wishes of the family.

more.description: HWANGE — Kamativi Mining Company (KMC) is accelerating a shift toward inclusive local economic development, awarding contracts to community-based businesses as part of a broader strategy to embed value within the Hwange District’s emerging lithium economy. more.title: ZIMRA Opens Penalty-Free Tax Disclosure Window to Boost Compliance and Formalise Economy
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) has launched a nationwide voluntary disclosure programme, offering taxpayers a limited window to regularise outstanding tax obligations without facing penalties or prosecution, as authorities intensify efforts to broaden compliance and formalise economic activity.
